_meta.note: event_context is the bounded raw-frame retrieval tool. It is the only MCP path in this batch through which frame text reaches the LLM. before/after/max_bytes are optional client hints clamped by daemon caps. TB-8/TB-11: bucket_id and event_id carry opaque-id format notes (copy verbatim, e.g. bkt_/evt_<32hex>); the success body carries frames OR an empty frames list with a typed unavailable_reason (NoPointer/SyntheticEvent/AnchorEvicted) -- that is a success shape, not a JSON-RPC error. US5/FR-040: bucket_id is OPTIONAL -- omit it to resolve the owning bucket from event_id alone (event ids are globally unique). A supplied bucket_id that contradicts the event's real bucket is an error (EventNotFound), never silently corrected; an unknown event errors identically in both addressing modes.

invariants: event_id is required; bucket_id is optional (US5/FR-040 id-alone resolution)
a supplied bucket_id that contradicts the event's real bucket errors (EventNotFound), never silently corrected
an unknown event errors identically (EventNotFound) in both addressing modes
bucket_id and event_id descriptions carry bkt_/evt_ opaque-id format notes (TB-8)
unavailable_reason is a success-body field, not a JSON-RPC error (TB-11)
frame text is bounded by daemon caps and returned only from event_context
